slides.title: Natural Wonders and Conservation
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 6: Natural Wonders and Conservation. Welcome to Unit 6: Natural Wonders and Conservation! In this unit, you will explore the extraordinary landscapes of East Africa and beyond, discovering how mountains, valleys, rivers, and wildlife shape the world around us. You will read an original adventure story called "The Citadel of Wind and Stone," set in the dramatic highlands of the Horn of Africa, where a group of young people must work together, make difficult decisions, and learn the meaning of true leadership. Through this story, you will encounter powerful vocabulary, practise important grammar structures, and develop your speaking and writing skills. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use comparative and superlative forms of adjectives correctly, including irregular forms like better, worse, best, and worst
Form and use comparative adverb structures to compare actions (for example, "She completed the task more quickly than he did")
Understand and use pronouns and quantifiers such as anyone, nothing, few, and all in your writing and speech
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use comparative and superlative forms of adjectives correctly, including irregular forms like better, worse, best, and worst. you will also form and use comparative adverb structures to compare actions (for example, "she completed the task more quickly than he did"). you will also understand and use pronouns and quantifiers such as anyone, nothing, few, and all in your writing and speech. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: atrocity, destination, desist, deter, authority, anarchy. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Natural Wonders and Conservation: source text 1
Natural Wonders and Conservation: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Natural Wonders and Conservation: source text 1 and Natural Wonders and Conservation: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: How to Form Comparatives
bullets: The way you form a comparative depends on the length of the adjective: One-syllable adjectives: Add -er to the end. Example: tall becomes taller, fast...
slides.narration: The first language focus is How to Form Comparatives. The way you form a comparative depends on the length of the adjective: One-syllable adjectives: Add -er to the end. Example: tall becomes taller, fast becomes faster, old becomes older. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Comparing Natural Wonders. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
